creator: vbasiccd@msn.com
text:
That is interesting. The only QT error I receive is the following, yet Orange continues to run.  My guess is that on my computer, I compiled everything from ports, customizing several config settings to my preferences.  It is possible that one or more of my adjustments prevented the QT error you experienced.

Qt: Session management error: None of the authentication protocols specified are supported

When I attempt to load a CSV file, I see this error.

QKqueueFileSystemWatcherEngine::addPaths: open: No such file or directory


I decided to try installing the latest version of Orange inside a Python virtual environment, as instructed here: https://pypi.org/project/Orange3

Before creating the virtual environment, I first confirmed that I had the following ports installed:

devel/py-virtualenv
devel/py-pip

The "pip install orange3" command revealed quite a few dependencies, most of which I already had installed.  I wanted to minimize the dependencies that pip needed to install, so after studying the output, I compiled the following dependency list:

x11-toolkits/py-AnyQt
devel/py-qtconsole
net/py-pyzmq
misc/py-xgboost
graphics/py-graphviz
graphics/py-plotly
www/py-requests-cache
math/py-baycomp
math/py-bottleneck
textproc/py-chardet
www/py-httpx
devel/py-joblib
security/py-keyring
security/py-keyrings.alt
math/py-matplotlib
math/py-networkx
math/py-numpy
math/py-openTSNE
textproc/py-openpyxl
devel/py-packaging
math/py-pandas
textproc/py-pygments
graphics/py-pyqtgraph
math/py-python-louvain
devel/py-pyyaml
www/py-grequests
science/py-scikit-learn
science/py-scipy
misc/py-serverfiles
textproc/py-xlrd
textproc/py-xlsxwriter
devel/py-six
devel/py-anyio
security/py-certifi
www/py-httpcore
dns/py-idna
devel/py-sniffio
net/py-h11
math/py-contourpy
devel/py-cycler
print/py-fonttools
math/py-kiwisolver
graphics/py-pillow
devel/py-pyparsing
devel/py-python-dateutil
textproc/py-docutils
textproc/py-CommonMark
misc/py-dictdiffer
devel/py-qasync
devel/py-typing-extensions
devel/py-pytz
devel/py-tzdata
devel/py-threadpoolctl
devel/py-jaraco.classes
textproc/py-et_xmlfile
textproc/py-charset-normalizer
net/py-urllib3
devel/py-platformdirs
devel/py-more-itertools

There were more dependencies in the output, but those were sub-dependencies of other packages in the above list.  And it is possible that this list could be reduced, as there are probably more sub-dependencies.

This left two unresolved dependencies: AnyQT and catboost.  Notice that "x11-toolkits/py-AnyQt" is in the above list.  However, the version in the ports tree is older than Orange needs, yet pip was able to install the correct version in the virtual environment.  The other dependency, catboost, is where I hit roadblocks.  I noticed three dependencies, which are in the ports tree:

devel/cmake
devel/ninja
sysutils/conan

Catboost itself is not in the ports tree, and "pip install catboost" always failed for two reasons.  First, the max Clang version it supports is 17, yet I think the default version in FreeBSD 14.1 is 18.  I have llvm17 installed, yet setting the CC and CXX environment variables in the Python virtual environment were ignored.  I eventually discovered that the "catboost-1.2.5/catboost_all_src/build/toolchains/clang.toolchain" file always overwrote those variables with the default Clang version, 18 in this case, which ironically is not supported.  The other reason is the version of "sysutils/conan".  The ports tree has the newer 2.x version, whereas the cmake config of catboost is using depreciated Conan 1.x commands.

In the Python virtual environment, I was able to install Conan 1.65.0, yet since I could not specify the Clang version, catboost failed building.  Downloading the source tarball outside of the environment, I could edit the toolchain file to use Clang 17, yet building still failed because of the newer Conan version.

So, in summary, from my observations, the following is needed in order to update the "misc/orange3" port:

- update py-AnyQT to a newer 2.x version
- create a catboost port with the following patches:
   - modify the CC and CXX variables in the toolchain file to use a max supported version
   - adapt all the Conan commands from version 1.x to 2.x

That last catboost modification might be difficult.  Not sure, though.
